commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] No discrete code defect is left for an automated repair; the remaining blocker is maintainer live Telegram proof or an explicit proof override.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern found; the diff is limited to Telegram dispatch logic and colocated tests. (sha=c25851bec81fe082c45adf95d979c1dbd502a5c6)
commands.review_summary:
The PR broadens Telegram post-final reply suppression to hide text-only tool noise and core-tagged non-terminal final warnings while preserving ordinary final error warnings, media, and exec approvals, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +50. Total +59 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction is present for current head. The source path and new tests reproduce the intended dispatcher behavior synthetically, but the PR lacks a real Telegram transcript or artifact showing the before/after user-visible issue.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No discrete code defect is left for an automated repair; the remaining blocker is maintainer live Telegram proof or an explicit proof override.

target.title: fix(telegram): suppress post-final tool error noise
target.body:
## Summary
- Suppress text-only tool/progress noise once a Telegram final answer has started or delivered
- Preserve terminal `final` error warnings, media payloads, and exec approval prompts
- Suppress `final` error payloads only when core marks them as `nonTerminalToolErrorWarning`
- Add regression coverage for late tool noise suppression, final warning preservation, and tagged non-terminal final warning suppression

## Why
Agents often run exploratory commands/searches where a failure or empty result is expected, such as file searches that return no matches. The model can handle that internally and still produce a correct final answer. If those handled tool errors are delivered after the final reply, they are non-actionable noise for the user and make a successful turn look broken.

At the same time, core intentionally emits terminal `final` `isError` warnings for mutating write/exec failures when the assistant claimed success. Those must stay visible. This PR preserves those warnings and only drops post-final tool/progress noise or final warnings explicitly tagged by core as non-terminal.

## Due diligence
- Reviewed ClawSweeper/Codex P1 feedback on preserving post-final error finals
- Repaired the broad `payload.isError` suppression so terminal `final` warnings remain visible
- Kept the existing core mutating-tool warning contract intact
- Keyed final-error suppression to existing reply metadata: `nonTerminalToolErrorWarning`
- Resolved the inline review thread after pushing the repair
- Rebased onto `origin/main` `9d6e8b872a` and squashed into single commit `c25851bec8`

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: Telegram should hide non-actionable post-final tool/progress failures, while preserving terminal final write/exec warnings, media payloads, and exec approval prompts.
- Real environment tested: Live OpenClaw Telegram runtime in the `Openclaw debug` Telegram topic after maintainer/operator build and restart.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Keshav G confirmed from the live Telegram debug topic on 2026-06-03 13:06:19 IST / 07:36:19 UTC that the OpenClaw runtime was built and restarted after the PR was rebased and squashed to then-current head `1a59a77093f9`. On 2026-06-04, this branch was rebased again onto `origin/main` `9d6e8b872a` and force-pushed as current head `c25851bec8` with the same one-commit PR surface.
- Evidence after fix: Maintainer/operator confirmation was received in live Telegram: `Built and restarted` for head `1a59a77093f9`. Current local branch is `fix/suppress-post-final-tool-errors` at `c25851bec8`, matching the PR head after the latest rebase.
- Observed result after fix: The live Telegram runtime came back online after the 2026-06-03 restart, the code P1 remains resolved, the inline review thread is resolved, and latest Codex review found no major issues. The remaining ClawSweeper blocker is live Telegram behavior proof acceptance.
- What was not tested: I did not run a build, test, restart, Mantis desktop proof, or new live Telegram behavior probe after the 2026-06-04 rebase to `c25851bec8`, per operator instruction to ask before build/test/restart. The existing live proof does not include a Telegram transcript, recording, redacted runtime log, or artifact showing post-final non-terminal warning suppression and terminal warning preservation.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is maintainer/operator live Telegram confirmation, not a Mantis bot artifact. The earlier Mantis Telegram proof request could not run from this contributor account because `keshavbotagent` has read-only repository permission. A maintainer-triggered Mantis Telegram Desktop proof, equivalent redacted live Telegram artifact, or explicit maintainer proof override is still needed before ClawSweeper can clear the proof gate. The failed Mantis authorization log was:

```text
Actor keshavbotagent permission: read
Workflow requires write/maintain/admin access. Actor "keshavbotagent" has "read".
Resolve Mantis request: skipped
Run agentic native Telegram proof: skipped
```

## Verification
Previous local verification before the rebase/squash:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`

Post-squash maintainer/operator confirmation:
- Built and restarted live OpenClaw Telegram runtime on 2026-06-03 13:06:19 IST / 07:36:19 UTC.

Post-2026-06-04 rebase:
- Rebased onto `origin/main` `9d6e8b872a` and pushed current head `c25851bec8`.
- No build, test, or restart was run after this rebase because operator approval is required first.

## Current review state
- Code P1 is folded into current squashed head `c25851bec8`
- Inline review thread is resolved
- Latest Codex review found no major issues
- Latest ClawSweeper review still requires stronger real Telegram behavior proof
- Mantis proof request was attempted, but skipped due contributor permission
- Waiting on maintainer-triggered Mantis, equivalent live Telegram artifact, or explicit maintainer proof override before merge
